title: Stack & Topple
description: Toddler stacks soft blocks then knocks them down with glee.
subject: motor
benefit_explanation: Builds hand-eye coordination, cause-and-effect understanding, and grip strength.
instructions_for_parent: Sit on the floor with your toddler and model stacking 2-3 blocks before letting them try.
safety_warnings: Use only soft blocks; remove any with sharp edges.
step_1_instruction: Lay 6 soft blocks on the floor in front of your toddler.
step_2_instruction: Stack 2 blocks slowly. Cheer when they topple.
step_3_instruction: Let your toddler stack and knock down at their own pace.

title: Bubble Chase
description: Toddler chases bubbles, learning whole-body movement and tracking.
subject: motor
benefit_explanation: Trains visual tracking, gross-motor coordination, and joyful movement.
instructions_for_parent: Use a non-toxic bubble solution. Stay near the toddler outdoors.
safety_warnings: Bubbles can be slippery on hard surfaces — play on grass or rug.
step_1_instruction: Send a few bubbles into the air.
step_2_instruction: Encourage your toddler to chase, pat, and pop them.
step_3_instruction: Sit down together and count the last few bubbles.

title: Animal Sounds
description: Toddler matches stuffed animals to the sound each one makes.
subject: language
benefit_explanation: Builds first-word vocabulary and listening comprehension.
instructions_for_parent: Use exaggerated, friendly animal sounds and pause to let the toddler imitate.
safety_warnings: Avoid sounds that startle (no loud roars).
step_1_instruction: Place the animals in a row.
step_2_instruction: Pick one up and say its name + sound (e.g. cow + 'moo').
step_3_instruction: Wait for the toddler to imitate; cheer any attempt.

title: Sticker Page
description: Toddler peels stickers and places them on a paper shape.
subject: art
benefit_explanation: Develops pincer grip, focus, and self-expression.
instructions_for_parent: Sit beside your toddler. Use large, easy-peel stickers.
materials_needed: Paper with a printed shape outline|Large stickers
safety_warnings: Stickers are not edible — supervise closely.
step_1_instruction: Show the toddler the outline (circle, heart).
step_2_instruction: Demonstrate peeling one and placing it inside the shape.
step_3_instruction: Let the toddler fill the shape with their own choices.

title: Big & Little Sort
description: Toddler sorts objects into 'big' and 'little' piles.
subject: cognitive
benefit_explanation: Introduces comparative thinking and one-attribute classification.
instructions_for_parent: Use 6-8 obvious size pairs (big spoon vs little spoon, etc.).
safety_warnings: Choose unbreakable items only.
step_1_instruction: Place one 'big' and one 'little' pile divider in front.
step_2_instruction: Hand items one at a time; ask 'big or little?'
step_3_instruction: Cheer when finished and count each pile aloud.

title: Mirror Faces
description: Toddler mimics emotional faces in a mirror with a parent.
subject: social
benefit_explanation: Develops emotional vocabulary and empathy through facial mirroring.
instructions_for_parent: Use a safe mirror or your phone in selfie mode.
materials_needed: A small mirror or phone camera
safety_warnings: Hold the mirror so the toddler can't grab and drop it.
step_1_instruction: Sit cheek-to-cheek looking at the mirror.
step_2_instruction: Pull a happy, then sad, then surprised face. Name each.
step_3_instruction: Let the toddler make a face — name what you see.

title: Dough Squish
description: Toddler squishes, rolls, and pokes play dough.
subject: sensory
benefit_explanation: Sensory regulation + finger strength + creative exploration.
instructions_for_parent: Use a clean surface. Roll a snake yourself to spark imitation.
materials_needed: Non-toxic play dough (or homemade flour dough)
safety_warnings: Supervise — dough is not for eating.
step_1_instruction: Open the container together; hand the toddler a piece.
step_2_instruction: Squish it slowly between your palms. Make a sound.
step_3_instruction: Roll a snake; let the toddler poke it with one finger.

title: Where's the Penguin?
description: Hide a toy and let the toddler find it under a blanket.
subject: cognitive
instructions_for_parent: Use one small toy and a thin blanket.
materials_needed: A small soft toy|A thin blanket
safety_warnings: Keep the blanket short — never cover the toddler's face.
step_1_instruction: Hold up the toy and name it ('Penguin!').
step_2_instruction: Cover it with the blanket as the toddler watches.
step_3_instruction: Ask 'where's the penguin?' and lift the blanket together.

title: Roll the Ball
description: Toddler rolls a ball back and forth with a parent.
subject: motor
instructions_for_parent: Sit facing each other 2-3 feet apart on a soft surface.
safety_warnings: Avoid hard balls and slippery floors.
step_1_instruction: Sit on the floor facing each other.
step_2_instruction: Roll the ball gently toward the toddler.
step_3_instruction: Wait for them to roll it back — encourage with smiles.

title: Crayon Scribble
description: Toddler holds a chunky crayon and scribbles freely.
subject: art
benefit_explanation: Early mark-making, grip strength, and self-expression.
instructions_for_parent: Use chunky toddler crayons that fit a fist grip.
safety_warnings: Crayons should be non-toxic; supervise mouth.
step_1_instruction: Tape paper to the table so it doesn't slide.
step_2_instruction: Hold a crayon in a fist and scribble slowly.
step_3_instruction: Hand them a crayon — applaud any mark.

title: Story Time Snuggle
description: A short rhyming book read together with full attention.
subject: literacy
benefit_explanation: Builds vocabulary, attachment, and listening stamina.
instructions_for_parent: Pick a board book with rhymes and pictures. No screens.
safety_warnings: Avoid books with small parts that detach.
step_1_instruction: Get cosy on the couch or floor.
step_2_instruction: Read slowly, pointing at pictures.
step_3_instruction: Pause and ask 'what's this?' on every page.

title: Water Pouring
description: Toddler pours water between two cups over a tray.
subject: sensory
instructions_for_parent: Use a contained tray. Towel ready.
materials_needed: 2 small plastic cups|A shallow tray|A towel
safety_warnings: Use water only — never hot. Mop spills immediately.
step_1_instruction: Place the tray on a low table.
step_2_instruction: Pour water into the first cup; hand it to the toddler.
step_3_instruction: Encourage pouring back and forth; talk about full + empty.

title: One, Two, Toes
description: Count the toddler's toes during dressing or bath.
subject: math
benefit_explanation: Early counting + bonding during daily routines.
instructions_for_parent: Use a sing-song voice for the count.
safety_warnings: No safety concerns.
step_1_instruction: Sit them down and hold one foot.
step_2_instruction: Touch each toe and count 1, 2, 3, 4, 5.
step_3_instruction: Repeat on the other foot.

title: Sing the Greeting
description: Sing a hello song with simple gestures.
subject: social
benefit_explanation: Welcomes the toddler to the day; builds rhythm and ritual.
instructions_for_parent: Use the same melody every time so they anticipate it.
safety_warnings: No safety concerns.
step_1_instruction: Sit facing each other.
step_2_instruction: Sing 'Hello, hello, hello to you!' while waving.
step_3_instruction: Encourage a return wave or sing-back.

title: Color Hunt
description: Find five red things in the room.
subject: language
instructions_for_parent: Pick one color per round; switch colors next time.
safety_warnings: No safety concerns.
step_1_instruction: Say 'today's color is RED!'
step_2_instruction: Walk around together pointing at red things; name each.
step_3_instruction: Count the red things you found.

title: Soft Tunnel Crawl
description: Toddler crawls through a fabric tunnel.
subject: motor
instructions_for_parent: Hold the tunnel steady. Cheer from the other end.
materials_needed: A pop-up fabric tunnel or sheet over chairs
safety_warnings: Tunnel must be open at both ends — never sealed.
step_1_instruction: Position the tunnel on a clear floor.
step_2_instruction: Crawl through yourself first if needed.
step_3_instruction: Encourage them through; cheer at the exit.

title: Fruit Names
description: Name fruits one at a time during snack.
subject: language
benefit_explanation: Vocabulary building tied to a real daily moment.
instructions_for_parent: Use one or two fruits per session.
safety_warnings: Avoid choking-hazard sizes — slice grapes lengthwise.
step_1_instruction: Hold up an apple. Say 'apple.'
step_2_instruction: Wait for them to say or attempt the word.
step_3_instruction: Offer a small bite and name it again.

title: Goodbye Wave
description: Practice waving goodbye at every leaving moment.
subject: social
benefit_explanation: Develops social ritual, predictability, and emotional regulation around separation.
instructions_for_parent: Use the same words every time.
safety_warnings: No safety concerns.
step_1_instruction: Use the same warm tone every time.
step_2_instruction: Wave your hand visibly.
step_3_instruction: Cheer any attempt — even a head turn counts.

title: Soft Cymbal Pat
description: Tap two soft cymbals or pot lids together.
subject: art
instructions_for_parent: Use SOFT cymbals or fabric drums to avoid harsh sounds.
materials_needed: 2 soft cymbals or upturned plastic bowls
safety_warnings: Avoid metal cymbals — too loud.
step_1_instruction: Give one to the toddler, keep one yourself.
step_2_instruction: Tap yours; encourage them to tap theirs.
step_3_instruction: Tap a slow steady beat and watch them copy.

title: Bird Watch
description: Watch birds from a window and name what you see.
subject: science
benefit_explanation: Observation, patience, and first nature vocabulary.
instructions_for_parent: Best near a window with a feeder or tree.
safety_warnings: Make sure the toddler can't climb on furniture.
step_1_instruction: Sit at the window quietly.
step_2_instruction: Point at any bird. Whisper its color or movement.
step_3_instruction: Describe what you saw together once the bird is gone.

title: Cup Stacking
description: Stack 3 plastic cups upside-down.
subject: motor
instructions_for_parent: Choose lightweight plastic cups.
safety_warnings: Avoid glass or any hard cups.
step_1_instruction: Place cups upside down in front of the toddler.
step_2_instruction: Stack 2 yourself. Hand them the third.
step_3_instruction: Let them knock and reset; repeat.

title: Hello Mirror
description: Toddler waves to themselves in a full mirror.
subject: social
instructions_for_parent: Use a stable, mounted mirror.
safety_warnings: Mirror must be securely mounted.
step_1_instruction: Stand in front of the mirror.
step_2_instruction: Wave at the toddler in the mirror.
step_3_instruction: Watch them recognise and wave back.

title: Bath Pour
description: During bath, pour water from one cup to another.
subject: sensory
benefit_explanation: Sensory regulation + fine motor + routine joy.
instructions_for_parent: Make this a regular bath ritual.
safety_warnings: Never leave a toddler unattended in water.
step_1_instruction: Place 2 cups in the bath.
step_2_instruction: Pour from one to the other slowly.
step_3_instruction: Help them pour; cheer the splash.

title: Imitate the Animal
description: Crawl like a cat, hop like a bunny, walk like an elephant.
subject: motor
instructions_for_parent: Choose 2-3 animals per session.
safety_warnings: Move soft furniture away.
step_1_instruction: Say 'let's be cats!'
step_2_instruction: Crawl on hands and knees, slowly.
step_3_instruction: Switch animals every minute or two.

title: Sock Sort
description: Match pairs of socks fresh out of the dryer.
subject: cognitive
benefit_explanation: Matching skills + helping with real chores.
instructions_for_parent: Use 4-6 obvious pairs.
safety_warnings: No safety concerns.
step_1_instruction: Pour clean socks on the floor.
step_2_instruction: Pick one; hunt for its pair together.
step_3_instruction: Stack matched pairs in a tidy row.

title: Echo Song
description: Sing a phrase, toddler echoes it back.
subject: language
instructions_for_parent: Use very short phrases at first ('la la la').
safety_warnings: No safety concerns.
step_1_instruction: Sing a short phrase clearly.
step_2_instruction: Pause for the toddler to echo.
step_3_instruction: Cheer any attempt; lengthen the phrase next time.

title: Texture Tray
description: Touch 3 different textures (smooth, rough, soft) and name them.
subject: sensory
instructions_for_parent: Use safe everyday items — felt, sandpaper, smooth stone.
materials_needed: A small tray with 3 textured items
safety_warnings: All items must be larger than a fist (no choking).
step_1_instruction: Place tray in front of the toddler.
step_2_instruction: Run their finger over each and name 'smooth, rough, soft'.
step_3_instruction: Ask them to pick the rough one.

title: Door Knock
description: Practice knocking on a door before entering.
subject: social
instructions_for_parent: Practice on a closed door at home first.
safety_warnings: No safety concerns.
step_1_instruction: Stand outside a closed door.
step_2_instruction: Knock 3 times. Say 'we wait for an answer.'
step_3_instruction: Open and say hello to whoever (or whatever) is inside.

title: Push the Cart
description: Push a small toy cart back and forth.
subject: motor
benefit_explanation: Gross motor + spatial awareness + sustained engagement.
instructions_for_parent: Use a stable push-toy or laundry basket with weight.
safety_warnings: Make sure path is clear of hazards.
step_1_instruction: Place the cart in front of the toddler.
step_2_instruction: Push it slowly in a straight line.
step_3_instruction: Walk alongside as they push the cart.

title: Lullaby Wind-Down
description: Sing a slow lullaby before nap or bedtime.
subject: social
benefit_explanation: Calms the nervous system + creates predictable transitions.
instructions_for_parent: Use a low, slow voice with one consistent song.
safety_warnings: No safety concerns.
step_1_instruction: Dim lights and find a cosy spot.
step_2_instruction: Sing your chosen lullaby softly.
step_3_instruction: Hold or rock for a minute of quiet after the song.

title: Light Switch
description: Toddler practices turning a light on and off (with adult supervision).
subject: science
benefit_explanation: Cause-and-effect understanding plus electrical safety vocabulary.
instructions_for_parent: Lift the toddler to reach a low switch only. Never let them climb to switches.
safety_warnings: Adult must lift the toddler — no climbing on furniture.
step_1_instruction: Point at the light and the switch.
step_2_instruction: Lift the toddler; let them flip it once.
step_3_instruction: Say 'on' and 'off' as you do it together.

title: Sticky Note Hunt
description: Stick 5 large sticky notes around the room; toddler finds and collects them.
subject: motor
benefit_explanation: Visual scanning + movement + sense of accomplishment.
instructions_for_parent: Use bright, contrasting paper. Place at toddler-eye level.
safety_warnings: Avoid placing on hot surfaces or near stairs.
step_1_instruction: Place 5 sticky notes around the room.
step_2_instruction: Walk together; let the toddler peel each off.
step_3_instruction: Count the notes when finished.

title: Pillow Mountain
description: Build a pillow pile and let the toddler climb safely.
subject: motor
benefit_explanation: Whole-body strength + spatial planning + joyful risk.
instructions_for_parent: Use soft pillows only; stay within arm's reach.
safety_warnings: Adult supervision essential — never leave the toddler.
step_1_instruction: Pile pillows on a soft floor.
step_2_instruction: Stay close as the toddler climbs.
step_3_instruction: Topple and rebuild together.

title: Counting Steps
description: Count steps as the toddler climbs stairs (with adult holding hand).
subject: math
instructions_for_parent: Hold the toddler's hand on every step. Never let go.
safety_warnings: Adult must hold hand at all times.
step_1_instruction: Stand together at the bottom step.
step_2_instruction: Say one number per step.
step_3_instruction: Celebrate reaching the top.

title: Fruit Salad Help
description: Toddler helps wash fruit and place it in a bowl.
subject: science
instructions_for_parent: Use only soft, washed fruit. Adult slices.
safety_warnings: Never give toddler a knife. Adult slices only.
step_1_instruction: Rinse a strawberry under cool water together.
step_2_instruction: Pat the fruit dry with a towel.
step_3_instruction: Drop fruit into the bowl. Smile.

title: Pat-a-Cake
description: Sing the classic clapping rhyme together.
subject: art
instructions_for_parent: Sit facing each other. Use exaggerated movements.
safety_warnings: No safety concerns.
step_1_instruction: Sit close enough to touch hands.
step_2_instruction: Clap a slow rhythm, sing 'pat-a-cake'.
step_3_instruction: Help them clap; cheer attempts.

title: Shape Find
description: Find 3 circles in the room.
subject: math
instructions_for_parent: Pick one shape per round.
safety_warnings: No safety concerns.
step_1_instruction: Say 'circles today'.
step_2_instruction: Point at any round thing and name it.
step_3_instruction: Help the toddler find 2 more circles.

title: Yes / No Game
description: Ask silly yes/no questions and have toddler nod or shake their head.
subject: language
instructions_for_parent: Use silly questions to make it fun.
safety_warnings: No safety concerns.
step_1_instruction: Sit facing the toddler.
step_2_instruction: 'Is the sky purple?'
step_3_instruction: Celebrate any clear nod or head shake.

title: Mat Roll
description: Toddler rolls on a soft mat or rug.
subject: motor
instructions_for_parent: Use a flat soft surface; clear nearby furniture.
safety_warnings: No hard objects nearby.
step_1_instruction: Help toddler lie on their back on the mat.
step_2_instruction: Gently guide them to roll to their side.
step_3_instruction: Roll the other way; cheer.

title: Big Box Hideout
description: Toddler crawls in and out of a big cardboard box.
subject: motor
instructions_for_parent: Make sure the box is open and clean.
safety_warnings: Never close the box flaps on the toddler.
step_1_instruction: Place the box on a soft floor.
step_2_instruction: Climb in and out yourself to demonstrate.
step_3_instruction: Let the toddler crawl in, out, and around.

title: Match the Lid
description: Match plastic container lids to their containers.
subject: cognitive
instructions_for_parent: Use 3-5 obvious size pairs.
safety_warnings: All plastic must be food-safe and unbreakable.
step_1_instruction: Place containers and lids separately on the floor.
step_2_instruction: Pick a container; find its lid.
step_3_instruction: Hand them a container; cheer when they match.

title: Story Acting
description: Act out a simple story with stuffed animals.
subject: language
instructions_for_parent: Use a familiar story like 'The Three Bears'.
safety_warnings: No safety concerns.
step_1_instruction: Tell a short story together.
step_2_instruction: Assign each animal a role.
step_3_instruction: Move the animals and re-tell the story together.

title: Bath Boat
description: Float a small toy boat in the bath.
subject: science
instructions_for_parent: Adult must stay arm's length during bath.
materials_needed: A toy boat or empty bottle
safety_warnings: Never leave the toddler alone in water.
step_1_instruction: Place the boat on the water.
step_2_instruction: Help the toddler push it to make waves.
step_3_instruction: Press it under; let it pop back up. Talk about why.

title: Two-Step Dance
description: Step left then step right with the toddler holding hands.
subject: art
instructions_for_parent: Use a steady song they enjoy.
safety_warnings: Clear floor space.
step_1_instruction: Stand facing the toddler holding both hands.
step_2_instruction: Step left together; toddler follows.
step_3_instruction: Step right; repeat to a slow beat.

title: Dry Pasta Pour
description: Pour dry pasta between two cups over a tray.
subject: sensory
instructions_for_parent: Use raw, hard pasta; supervise mouth.
safety_warnings: Never leave toddler alone with small pieces.
step_1_instruction: Place pasta in one cup; tray underneath.
step_2_instruction: Pour into the second cup slowly.
step_3_instruction: Repeat the other way; cheer the sound.

title: Snack on a Plate
description: Toddler places snack pieces on their own plate.
subject: social
instructions_for_parent: Choose safe-sized snack pieces.
safety_warnings: Avoid choking-size pieces.
step_1_instruction: Hand the toddler an empty plate.
step_2_instruction: Offer 3 snack pieces to transfer.
step_3_instruction: Eat with them; talk about flavours.

title: Doorbell Sound
description: Practice a polite 'who is it?' when the doorbell rings.
subject: social
instructions_for_parent: Practice with imaginary doorbells first.
safety_warnings: Adult always opens; never let toddler open alone.
step_1_instruction: Say 'ding-dong!' yourself.
step_2_instruction: Prompt: 'who is it?'
step_3_instruction: Adult opens the door safely.

title: Toy Wash
description: Wash plastic toys in a small basin of water.
subject: sensory
instructions_for_parent: Use lukewarm water; minimal soap.
safety_warnings: Never leave water unattended.
step_1_instruction: Fill a small basin with warm water.
step_2_instruction: Show how to gently scrub a toy.
step_3_instruction: Line dried toys up on a towel.

title: Sock Puppet Hello
description: Make a sock puppet and have it say hi.
subject: art
instructions_for_parent: Use a clean old sock; no buttons.
safety_warnings: Avoid small buttons; just use the sock itself.
step_1_instruction: Slip the sock over your hand.
step_2_instruction: Open and close to make it speak.
step_3_instruction: Let the toddler wear it and greet you.

title: Open / Close
description: Toddler practices opening and closing containers with lids.
subject: motor
instructions_for_parent: Use containers with simple twist or snap lids.
safety_warnings: All containers must be empty and clean.
step_1_instruction: Hand the toddler a closed container.
step_2_instruction: Slowly demonstrate opening it.
step_3_instruction: Help close it; cheer success.

title: Find Your Shoes
description: Toddler finds their own shoes before going outside.
subject: social
instructions_for_parent: Always keep shoes in the same spot.
safety_warnings: Adult helps with actual putting-on.
step_1_instruction: Say 'where are your shoes?'
step_2_instruction: Wait for them to walk to the spot.
step_3_instruction: They bring the shoes to a sit-down spot.

title: Cup of Water Pour
description: Pour from a small pitcher into a cup.
subject: motor
instructions_for_parent: Use a tiny pitcher and a stable cup.
safety_warnings: Water only — never hot. Towel ready.
step_1_instruction: Set up on a tray to catch spills.
step_2_instruction: Show pouring slowly into a cup.
step_3_instruction: Hand over the pitcher with one hand on theirs.

title: Magnet Wall
description: Stick refrigerator magnets in a row or pattern.
subject: science
benefit_explanation: Cause-effect with magnetism + fine motor + design.
instructions_for_parent: Use large, child-safe magnets.
safety_warnings: Avoid small magnets — choking + ingestion danger.
step_1_instruction: Show a magnet sticking to the fridge.
step_2_instruction: Hand magnets one at a time.
step_3_instruction: Help arrange in a row or shape.

title: Soft Pillow Push
description: Push a pillow across the room as fast as possible.
subject: motor
instructions_for_parent: Use a clear floor lane.
safety_warnings: Clear floor of obstacles.
step_1_instruction: Put pillow at the start.
step_2_instruction: Toddler pushes pillow to the other side.
step_3_instruction: Bring it back and try again.

title: Eye Patch Pirate
description: Toddler wears a soft eye patch and walks like a pirate.
subject: art
benefit_explanation: Imagination + balance with reduced vision + giggles.
instructions_for_parent: Use a soft fabric patch with a stretchy band.
safety_warnings: Adult should stay close for balance.
step_1_instruction: Place the patch gently over one eye.
step_2_instruction: Hold hand and walk a few steps.
step_3_instruction: Take off and put back on with giggles.

title: Whisper Game
description: Whisper a one-word message into the toddler's ear.
subject: language
instructions_for_parent: Use very simple one-word messages.
safety_warnings: No safety concerns.
step_1_instruction: Sit cheek-to-cheek with the toddler.
step_2_instruction: Whisper 'banana' or another fun word.
step_3_instruction: Wait for them to whisper it back; cheer.

title: Wave to the Mirror
description: Greet yourself in a mirror — different waves each time.
subject: social
instructions_for_parent: Use a stable, mounted mirror.
safety_warnings: Mirror must be mounted securely.
step_1_instruction: Wave high; cheer.
step_2_instruction: Wave with just fingers.
step_3_instruction: Wave with both hands at once.

title: Banana Roll
description: Roll a banana between the floor and palm.
subject: motor
instructions_for_parent: Use a fresh whole banana; supervise mouth.
safety_warnings: Adult supervises eating.
step_1_instruction: Lay a banana between you on the floor.
step_2_instruction: Roll it gently between palms.
step_3_instruction: Peel and share when done.

title: Quiet Cuddle
description: Sit close in silence for one minute.
subject: social
instructions_for_parent: No phones or screens. Just presence.
safety_warnings: No safety concerns.
step_1_instruction: Sit on the couch or floor together.
step_2_instruction: Take slow breaths; close your eyes.
step_3_instruction: End by whispering 'I love you' to the toddler.

title: Spoon Tap Beat
description: Tap two metal spoons together to a slow beat.
subject: art
instructions_for_parent: Use spoons that aren't loud enough to startle.
safety_warnings: Stay close; supervise mouth.
step_1_instruction: Give the toddler one spoon, keep one.
step_2_instruction: Tap yours; they tap theirs.
step_3_instruction: Speed up; slow down; giggle.
